text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint town nestled between rolling hills and shimmering lakes, there lived a quirky fox named Fergus. Fergus loved the simple pleasures—snuggling under the night sky, stalking moths with his eagle eyes, and gathering fallen berries.

One day, as Fergus was walking through the park, he noticed a group of teenagers building a bonfire. They seemed absorbed, but Fergus sensed curiosity. He sidled up beside them, his bushy tail twitching cheerfully.

"Hey, do you reckon you could use a friend? I've always wanted to see how big a fire can get," Fergus inquired.

The teenagers looked up, surprised. "We're on our way to a luau! Have you met poi?" one asked, gesturing at Fergus.

"No, never had. I'm Fergus," he said, introducing himself.
